title: ScanMesh: Artist-Style Meshes from Real 3D Scans via Self-Supervised Encoders
teaser: Generate clean, artist-style meshes directly from noisy real-world 3D scans by combining a strong self-supervised point cloud encoder (Utonia) with an autoregressive mesh decoder, trained entirely on simulated scans of synthetic objects.
abstract: Autoregressive mesh generators such as MeshAnything produce clean, artist-style meshes from point clouds of synthetic objects, but degrade sharply on real-world 3D scans, which carry sensor noise, occlusion, non-uniform density, and out-of-distribution geometry. We propose to close this synthetic-real gap not by retraining the mesh decoder on real data — which is impractical because paired (real scan, artist mesh) data does not exist at scale — but by replacing the encoder with a strong self-supervised point cloud encoder (Utonia) trained across diverse synthetic and real point clouds. Our hypothesis is that Utonia's representations are sufficiently invariant to the synthetic-real gap that an autoregressive mesh decoder trained only on (virtual scan, artist mesh) pairs of ShapeNet objects will transfer to real scans at inference time. We first measure the synthetic-real gap in the Utonia feature space across synthetic shapes, virtual scans, and real 3D scans. We then train the full pipeline and evaluate on real-scan benchmarks, comparing against MeshAnything baselines on both geometric accuracy and mesh-quality metrics, and analyzing where the synthetic-real transfer succeeds and fails.
milestones: Literature review (autoregressive mesh generation, self-supervised point cloud encoders, synthetic-to-real transfer for 3D shape tasks); build a virtual-scan pipeline with a realistic noise model (Kinect/RealSense-style simulation with multi-view fusion); generate paired (virtual scan, artist mesh) data from ShapeNet across selected categories.

Probe the Utonia feature space across synthetic shapes, virtual scans, and real 3D scans for same- and cross-category objects; quantify the synthetic-real gap via retrieval, clustering, and distance-distribution analysis; decide whether to use the encoder frozen or with light fine-tuning.

Train a GPT-style autoregressive mesh decoder on (virtual scan, artist mesh) pairs, conditioned on Utonia features; ablate encoder configuration (frozen vs. fine-tuned, intermediate vs. final features) and conditioning interface.

Evaluate on real-scan benchmarks (e.g., Scan2CAD-aligned ScanNet objects, or a curated subset) against MeshAnything-class baselines using both geometric metrics (Chamfer, F-score) and mesh-quality metrics (triangle count, manifoldness, visual quality); analyze failure modes by category, scan quality, and object complexity.

(Stretch) Investigate test-time strategies for handling out-of-distribution real scans — novel categories, severe occlusion, scale mismatches — and report whether the encoder-invariance hypothesis holds in the open-set regime.

prerequisites.label: Advanced Deep Learning for Computer Vision: Visual Computing (ADL4CV)

references.label: Utonia: Toward One Encoder for All Point Clouds
MeshAnything V2: Artist-Created Mesh Generation with Adjacent Mesh Tokenization
MeshGPT: Generating Triangle Meshes with Decoder-Only Transformers
PolyGen: An Autoregressive Generative Model of 3D Meshes
PatchComplete: Learning Multi-Resolution Patch Priors for 3D Shape Completion on Unseen Categories
